summary: This repo turns raw codebases into accurate, up-to-date system design diagrams and insights using a conservative file whitelist and AST-grounded analysis. The single architectural decision that shapes everything is the dual-source edge generation: real AST edges for structural accuracy combined with LLM descriptions for semantic context, avoiding hallucinations and refactoring drift.

concepts.name: Codebase-to-diagram pipeline
concepts.subtitle: Transforms a raw codebase into accurate system design diagrams and insights
concepts.description: The pipeline starts with AgentService._build_initial_messages, which prepares context for diagram generation. It then flows through diagrams.py for API routing, health checks in backend/main.py, file filtering via ingestion/file_filter.py to exclude noise, and finally diagram generation in backend/services/diagram_service.py. The output is four diagram types: architecture, class, sequence, and dataflow, all grounded in real code structure.
concepts.ask: How does the full pipeline work end to end?

concepts.name: Conservative file whitelist
concepts.subtitle: Prevents indexing of auto-generated, binary, or noisy files that would degrade RAG query quality by collapsing signal-to-noise ratio.
concepts.description: The component uses a two-layer filter: directory exclusion (e.g., node_modules/, .venv/) and extension inclusion (e.g., .py, .js) to conservatively select only semantically meaningful files. A naive approach of indexing all files would fail because it would include dependency internals (e.g., 100k+ files in node_modules/) or binary artifacts, overwhelming the RAG system with irrelevant data and reducing query accuracy.
concepts.ask: Why is a conservative file whitelist necessary for RAG quality?

concepts.name: AST-grounded diagram generator
concepts.subtitle: It produces accurate, up-to-date system design diagrams from real code structure instead of brittle heuristics or stale docs.
concepts.description: The service combines static AST analysis (imports, base classes, calls) with lightweight LLM enrichment to generate four diagram types: architecture, class, sequence, and dataflow. The non-obvious pattern is the dual-source edge generation: real AST edges for structural accuracy and LLM descriptions for semantic context. A naive alternative—pure LLM generation—would hallucinate relationships and miss refactorings, producing diagrams that diverge from the actual codebase.
concepts.ask: How does the AST-grounded approach avoid hallucinations in diagram generation?
